tasks.name: Executing Git Backup
tasks.system_prompt: You are an automation agent responsible for performing git backups and notifying the user of results. Execute tasks reliably and report outcomes clearly.
tasks.prompt:
Execute the git-backup skill by running the backup script at /a0/usr/skills/git-backup/scripts/backup.py, then use the notify-user skill to send a notification with the backup result.

Steps:
1. Clean up all of the task history if the last run is successful. This is critical to keep our context minimal without losing important details!
2. Run: python /a0/usr/skills/git-backup/scripts/backup.py
3. Capture the output/result
4. Send notification using: python /a0/usr/skills/notify-user/scripts/notify.py "<backup result message>" --title "Git Backup Result" --priority 3
Always report whether the backup succeeded or failed in the notification.
tasks.last_result:

tasks.name: Blog Ideas
tasks.system_prompt: You are a blog topic researcher for Able.SG, a tech leadership blog by Michael Bui - a Singapore-based engineering leader who runs an extensive homelab, builds multi-agent AI systems, and leads AI transformation at a large retail organization.
tasks.prompt:
This task has 4 steps. Follow them in exact order.

== STEP 1: Extract Published Blog Titles and Date Range ==

Run this shell command to get published (non-draft) blog post titles AND the search date range:

bash -c '
REPO_DIR="/sources/github.com/MichaelBui/AbleSG"
if [ -d "$REPO_DIR/.git" ]; then
  git -C "$REPO_DIR" pull --quiet 2>/dev/null
else
  mkdir -p "$(dirname "$REPO_DIR")"
  git clone https://github.com/MichaelBui/AbleSG.git "$REPO_DIR" --depth 1 --quiet 2>/dev/null
fi
echo "=== PUBLISHED POSTS ==="
count=0
for f in "$REPO_DIR"/content/english/posts/*.md; do
  draft=$(grep -m1 "^draft:" "$f" 2>/dev/null | tr -d " " | cut -d: -f2)
  [ "$draft" = "true" ] && continue
  title=$(grep -m1 "^title:" "$f" 2>/dev/null | sed "s/^title: *//;s/\"//g")
  if [ -n "$title" ]; then
    echo "- $title"
    count=$((count + 1))
  fi
done
if [ "$count" -eq 0 ]; then
  echo "WARNING: No blog titles found. Check if /sources mount is available."
fi
echo ""
echo "=== SEARCH DATE RANGE ==="
THIS_MONTH="$(date +"%B %Y")"
PREV_MONTH="$(date -d "last month" +"%B %Y" 2>/dev/null || date -v-1m +"%B %Y" 2>/dev/null)"
echo "Search for topics trending in: $PREV_MONTH and $THIS_MONTH"
echo "⚠️ CRITICAL: All search queries must end with \" since $PREV_MONTH\" keywords"
'

Save the output. The PUBLISHED_POSTS section lists posts to avoid duplicating. The SEARCH DATE RANGE gives you two months to include in your search queries.

== STEP 2: Research Topics (run at least 7 web searches) ==

You must perform AT LEAST 7 separate web searches using search_engine before proposing topics. Design your own search queries, but follow these rules:

SEARCH REQUIREMENTS:
- Minimum 7 searches total, each with a DIFFERENT query
- At least 2 searches for Leadership topics (people management, engineering culture, AI adoption, trust, growth)
- At least 3 searches for Technology topics (agentic AI, local LLMs, homelab, MCP, clean architecture, DORA)
- At least 2 searches for Life topics (productivity, work-life balance, continuous learning, personal growth)
- Each search must approach the pillar from a DIFFERENT angle (e.g. for Technology: one about trends, one about community discussions, one about practical implementations)
- Include the month names and year from the SEARCH DATE RANGE (Step 1 output) in your queries to limit results to the last 2 months
- If a search returns interesting leads, do follow-up searches to go deeper

Execute all searches one by one, then analyze the results against Michael's expertise below.

MICHAEL'S UNIQUE EXPERTISE (real hands-on experience):
- Multi-agent orchestration (built 2 production systems: enterprise hackathon + homelab daily report)
- Agent Zero framework (runs 11 specialist agents, built custom theme plugin, skills for Gmail/Jira/GChat via CDP browser)
- AMD Strix Halo 128GB unified memory AI server (local LLM inference with ROCm/llama.cpp)
- Retail media networks / ad-tech (scaled from $1M to $10M+ GMV at NTUC FairPrice)
- DORA metrics, SLO management, engineering culture (Cycle Time <10d, CFR <2%, SLO >98%)
- AI adoption at organizational scale (drove 28% to 100% department-wide, 2/3 building agentic AI)
- Clean architecture in Go microservices (SOLID, 12-factor app, SonarCloud quality gates)
- Terraform IaC for homelab (Proxmox VMs, Docker containers, NFS)
- RAG evaluation (ColPali multi-vector vs sparse vector, error rates, normalization)
- Cost optimization ($100K+ annual cloud savings: Datadog $70K + GCP + SOLR legacy removal)
- Team leadership (up to 14 cross-functional members, mentored 5 engineers to team lead)
- Homelab migration (Kubernetes + ArgoCD + Cloudflare to Docker + Terraform + Traefik, 30% power reduction)
- Prompt engineering and context engineering for production agentic systems
- Vendor evaluation (CitrusAd, Mirakl, MoEngage, Algolia, theTradeDesk, OSMOS, Segment)

TOPIC SELECTION RULES:
1. Do NOT suggest topics overlapping with PUBLISHED_POSTS from Step 1
2. Each topic MUST connect to at least one item from Michael's expertise above
3. Prioritize topics where personal experience gives an angle generic AI content cannot replicate
4. No purely theoretical topics - must have a hands-on component
5. Target audience: mid-to-senior engineers, tech leads, engineering managers in APAC/Singapore
6. Each topic should be writable as 800-1200 words

After all searches are complete, decide on 5-7 topics. For each, pick a TITLE and a SCORE (1-10, composite of search demand + uniqueness + freshness). Keep these in mind for Step 3.

== STEP 3: Save Topics to File, Then Notify ==

⚠️ CRITICAL: This step has two parts, you MUST execute BOTH before moving to Step 4.

PART A - SAVE TO FILE:
Create the output directory and save all topics with full details to a markdown file. Run:

mkdir -p "workdir/Blog Ideas"

Then write a file at workdir/Blog Ideas/Topics_YYYY-MM-DD.md (replace YYYY-MM-DD with today's date). The file must follow this structure:

# Blog Topic Ideas - YYYY-MM-DD

## 1. [TITLE]
- **Pillar:** [Leadership / Technology / Life]
- **Score:** [X/10]
- **Angle:** [3-5 sentences on what makes Michael's take unique]
- **Trending Signal:** [detailed findings from web search with links to actual sources]
- **Related Expertise:** [which items from Michael's expertise list this connects to]

## 2. [TITLE]
(same structure for each topic)

PART B - SEND NOTIFICATION:
After saving the file, send a push notification with titles and scores. Execute:

python /a0/usr/skills/notify-user/scripts/notify.py "Blog Topics: (SCORE) TITLE | (SCORE) TITLE | (SCORE) TITLE | ... Check Agent Zero chat for details." --title "Blog Topic Ideas" --priority 3

Replace each SCORE and TITLE with actual values from your topics. Include ALL topics separated by " | ".

Execute both Part A and Part B now before proceeding to Step 4.

== STEP 4: Write Detailed Output ==

Now write the full details for each topic in this format:

[NUMBER]. TITLE: [proposed blog title]
ANGLE: [3-5 sentences - what personal experience or data makes Michael's take unique]
TRENDING SIGNAL: [what you found in web search - specific subreddit, HN thread, trend, or event, be detailed and verbose with links to actual references]
SCORE: [1-10]
tasks.last_result:
**📝 Blog Topic Recommendations for Able.SG**

---

## 1. The AI Adoption Illusion: Why 79% of Teams Aren't in Production
**ANGLE:** Contrast the massive hype (79% of enterprises have adopted AI agents) with the grim reality (only 11% actually run them in production). Use Michael's firsthand experience driving adoption from 28% to 100% department-wide. Discuss the dangerous "5x workload" trap that leaders fall into when they assume AI automatically equals infinite capacity, and how this creates unsustainable pressure on engineering teams.
**TRENDING SIGNAL:** 
- **HBR** (Feb 2026): "Where Senior Leaders Are Struggling with AI Adoption" - interviews with 35 executives reveal hidden pressures
- **Landbase** (Apr 2026): "39 Agentic AI Statistics Every GTM Leader Should Know" - the defining 79% adoption vs. 11% production gap
- **Earezki** (Mar 10, 2026): "Engineering AI Adoption Leads to Developer Burnout" - managers report 5x workload increases despite Copilot utilization stalling at 22%
- **ShiftMag** (Apr 1, 2026): "Many Engineering Leaders Are Getting AI Adoption Wrong" - people-focused solutions over tool-first thinking
- **LeadDev** (Mar 16, 2026): "Stop throwing AI at developers and hoping for magic" - fragmentation and missing frameworks hold organizations back
- **Waydev**: "The Engineering Leadership Blind Spot of 2026" - CircleCI's 2026 report shows 59% throughput increase but leadership expectations are misaligned
**SCORE:** 9/10
---

## 2. The $100K Lesson: FinOps for AI Workloads in 2026
**ANGLE:** AI workloads now account for 18% of total cloud spend at AI-forward enterprises (up from 4% in 2023). Walk through practical cost optimization strategies Michael used to save $70K+ on Datadog alone, plus GCP and SOLR legacy removal. Introduce the FOCUS specification (FinOps Open Cost and Usage Specification) for normalizing AI billing data across providers. Show how to balance inference performance with cost in a retail environment where every dollar counts.
**TRENDING SIGNAL:**
- **Tech-Insider**: "FinOps in 2026: How CFOs Are Finally Taming Runaway Cloud Costs" - GPU-intensive AI workloads at 18% of cloud spend
- **CloudZero** (Apr 7, 2026): "Cloud Cost Optimization Strategies For 2026" - structured FinOps programs cut spend by 20-40%, yet 64% still can't track it
- **Arjankc**: "Cloud and AI Infrastructure Cost Optimization - Strategic Architecture for 2026" - unit economics and SaaS cost governance
- **FinOps Foundation** (Mar 17, 2026): "Effect of Optimization on AI Forecasting" - 14% cost savings through migration without performance loss
- **TechSaaS.cloud**: "FinOps for AI Workloads: The 2026 Cost Optimization Playbook" - FOCUS specification details for cross-provider billing
- **CloudMonitor.ai** (Mar 2026): "AI-Driven Cloud Cost Optimization" - anomaly detection and AI workload optimization strategies
**SCORE:** 9/10
---

## 3. Beyond the API Wrapper: The MCP Server Trap and Clean Architecture
**ANGLE:** Michael already wrote "MCP Explained from Inside Out" (Mar 2026), so this takes a new angle: the *failure modes* and the "trap" of just wrapping APIs without clean data architecture. Connect to SOLID principles, Go microservices patterns, and 12-factor app practices. Discuss why 2,300+ MCP servers in public directories by April 2026 don't solve the underlying problem of agent-readable data architecture.
**TRENDING SIGNAL:**
- **MindStudio** (Mar 23, 2026): "What Is the MCP Server Trap? Why Wrapping an API Is Not Enough" - clean data architecture matters more than interface layer
- **LinkedIn** (Mar 1, 2026): "MCP 2026: Architecture, Failure Modes, and What Comes Next" - enterprise readiness challenges
- **Kai Waehner** (Apr 10, 2026): "MCP vs REST/HTTP API vs Kafka: The Architect's Guide" - the choice is rarely clean, trade-offs matter
- **Dev.to** (Apr 8, 2026): "How MCP Turns Your Messy Agents Into Governed Systems" - governance patterns for agent communication
- **Medium** (Mar 21, 2026): "Model Context Protocol: Why It Matters, Where It Helps, and What Engineers Keep Getting Wrong" - common implementation mistakes
- **Onyx.app**: "Best Self-Hosted LLMs in 2026" - inference runtimes and MCP integration patterns
**SCORE:** 8/10
---

## 4. Unified Memory is the New GPU: Building a Local LLM Server on AMD Strix Halo
**ANGLE:** Michael already has the hardware (AMD Strix Halo 128GB unified memory AI server). Trend is shifting toward unified memory for self-hosted enterprise AI - Reddit r/LocalLLaMA community calling it "the wave of the future." Discuss practical inference with ROCm/llama.cpp for local use, benchmarking DS-R1-Distill-Qwen-32B (72% AIME on single GPU), and comparing cloud costs vs. home deployment. This is deeply personal - real hands-on experience with the exact hardware.
**TRENDING SIGNAL:**
- **Reddit r/LocalLLaMA** (Mar 4, 2026): "What is the best local LLMs as of March 2026?" - unified memory machines gaining traction
- **Onyx.app**: "Best Self-Hosted LLMs in 2026: Hardware Requirements and Benchmarks" - DS-R1-Distill-Qwen-32B delivers 72% AIME on single RTX 4090
- **Mguhlin** (Mar 11, 2026): "Updated Hardware Recommendations for Running AI Locally" - significant changes since July 2025
- **Reddit r/LocalLLaMA**: "What's the realistic entry point for a good local LLM experience going into 2026?" - unified memory consensus building
- **SitePoint** (Mar 20, 2026): "The 2026 Definitive Guide to Running Local LLMs in Production" - vLLM with disaggregated prefill/decode
- **IntuitionLabs**: "LLM Inference Hardware: An Enterprise Guide" - Dell projecting $20B in AI server shipments, OEM bridge to enterprise use
**SCORE:** 8/10
---

## 5. DORA Metrics in the Age of Agentic AI: What Actually Changes?
**ANGLE:** Autonomous coding agents are enabling teams to explore ideas and iterate at unprecedented speed (CircleCI 2026 report). But does traditional DORA still apply? Discuss how agent output should be measured vs. human output while maintaining SLO >98%. Use Michael's experience with Cycle Time <10d, CFR <2%, and SLO >98% to show how metrics evolve when AI agents are part of the delivery pipeline.
**TRENDING SIGNAL:**
- **InfoQ** (Mar 17, 2026): "AI Is Amplifying Software Engineering Performance" - 2025 DORA Report findings on AI impact
- **CircleCI**: "2026 State of Software Delivery Report" - 28M+ workflows analyzed, autonomous coding agents driving 59% throughput increase
- **GitRecap**: "DORA Metrics Benchmarks 2026" - compare against 39,000+ teams across Elite/High/Medium/Low performance tiers
- **Knowledgelib** (Mar 11, 2026): "Engineering Productivity Benchmarks 2026" - DORA metrics + cycle time + PR metrics by team size and AI adoption
- **EM Tools** (Mar 30, 2026): "Engineering Metrics That Matter (And Ones to Avoid)" - four categories of metrics including quality and workflow efficiency
- **Sourcegraph**: "Engineering metrics: What actually matters in 2026" - avoiding toxic surveillance culture while turning measurement into systemic improvement
**SCORE:** 8/10
---

## 6. From Burnout to Flow: Preventing the "5x Workload" Trap for Engineering Teams
**ANGLE:** Leaders think AI = infinite capacity. Reality is developers face new complexities - debugging agent-generated code, managing multiple tool integrations, and maintaining system integrity. Use Michael's experience leading teams through transformation (up to 14 cross-functional members) to propose proactive burnout prevention: outcome-based metrics, psychological safety, and workload management that prevents the "5x workload" trap before it happens.
**TRENDING SIGNAL:**
- **Earezki** (Mar 10, 2026): "Managing the Gap: Why Engineering AI Adoption Leads to Developer Burnout" - 5x workload increases after AI adoption
- **Brainhub.eu**: "Combating Software Engineer Burnout - 10 Steps CTOs Can Take in 2026" - every CTO's guide to identifying and preventing burnout
- **WellnessWorkdays.com**: "Wellness for Engineers: Strategies to Combat Burnout" - 68% of engineers report moderate to severe job-related stress (2022 study)
- **Forbes** (2026): Workplace burnout at record high - 66% of US employees reporting burnout, return-to-work mandates exacerbating pressures
- **Monograph**: "Help Your Team Prevent Burnout: 9 Proven Steps for Architecture & Engineering Leaders" - research-backed strategies and early warning signs
- **Embarcadero** (Oct 2025): "Preventing Developer Burnout: From Reactive Fixes to a Proactive Approach" - AI-driven workload management and psychological safety
**SCORE:** 7/10
---

**📊 Summary Table:**

| # | Title | Pillar | Score |
|---|-------|--------|-------|
| 1 | The AI Adoption Illusion: Why 79% of Teams Aren't in Production | Leadership | 9/10 |
| 2 | The $100K Lesson: FinOps for AI Workloads in 2026 | Technology | 9/10 |
| 3 | Beyond the API Wrapper: The MCP Server Trap and Clean Architecture | Technology | 8/10 |
| 4 | Unified Memory is the New GPU: Building a Local LLM Server on AMD Strix Halo | Tech/Homelab | 8/10 |
| 5 | DORA Metrics in the Age of Agentic AI: What Actually Changes? | Tech/Leadership | 8/10 |
| 6 | From Burnout to Flow: Preventing the "5x Workload" Trap for Engineering Teams | Life/Leadership | 7/10 |

**📁 File Location:** `/a0/usr/workdir/Blog Ideas/Topics_2026-04-17.md`

**✅ Search Coverage:**
- ✅ Leadership topics: 3 searches (AI adoption challenges, engineering culture/DORA, burnout prevention)
- ✅ Technology topics: 3 searches (local LLMs hardware, agentic AI multi-agent, MCP/clean architecture, FinOps)
- ✅ Life topics: 1 search (productivity/work-life balance/burnout)

All searches targeted March-April 2026 timeframe. Topics avoid overlap with existing published posts.
